back_translation: Hannah prayed and said, 'My heart rejoices in the LORD; my horn is raised up in the LORD; my mouth is wide against my enemies, because I rejoice in your salvation.'
There is no Holy One like the LORD, for there is none besides you, and there is no Rock like our God.
Do not multiply speaking proud, proud — let not arrogance come out from your mouth, for a God of knowledge is the LORD, and by him deeds are weighed.
The bows of the mighty are broken, but those who stumble are girded with strength.
Those full of bread hire themselves out, but the hungry cease — until the barren bears seven, and the one with many children languishes.
The LORD kills and gives life; he brings down to Sheol and brings up.
The LORD makes poor and makes rich; he humbles, also he exalts.

He raises from the dust the poor, from the ash heap he lifts the needy, to seat them with princes, and a throne of glory he causes them to inherit; for the LORD's are the pillars of the earth, and he has set upon them the world.
The feet of his faithful ones he guards, but the wicked in darkness are silenced; for not by strength shall a man prevail.

The LORD — those who contend with him shall be broken — against them in the heavens he will thunder. The LORD will judge the ends of the earth, and he will give strength to his king, and he will raise the horn of his anointed.
Elkanah went to Ramah to his house, and the boy was ministering the LORD before the face of Eli the priest.
And the sons of Eli were sons of Belial — they did not know the LORD.
And the custom of the priests with the people: every man offering a sacrifice, the priest's young man would come at the boiling of the meat, with a three-toothed fork in his hand.
He would thrust into the pan or into the kettle, or into the cauldron or into the pot — all that the fork brought up the priest would take in it. So they did to all Israel who came there at Shiloh.
Also before they would burn the fat as smoke, the priest's young man would come and say to the man sacrificing, 'Give meat to roast for the priest — he will not take from you boiled meat, only raw.'

When the man said to him, 'They shall surely burn as smoke the fat today — and take for yourself as your soul desires,' he would say to him, 'No — for now you shall give, and if not, I will take by force.'
And the sin of the young men was very great before the face of the LORD, for the men despised the offering of the LORD.
And Samuel was ministering the face of the LORD — a boy girded with a linen ephod.
A little robe his mother would make him, and bring it to him from days to days, in her going up with her husband to sacrifice the sacrifice of the days.
Eli would bless Elkanah and his wife, and say, 'May the LORD establish for you seed from this woman in place of the asked one that she asked to the LORD.' And they would go to his place.
For the LORD visited Hannah, and she conceived and bore three sons and two daughters, and the boy Samuel grew up with the LORD.
Eli was very old, and he heard all that his sons were doing to all Israel — and how they lay with the women who served at the door of the tent of meeting.
He said to them, 'Why do you do things like these — for I am hearing your wicked words from all this people?'
'Do not, my sons, for not good is the report that I am hearing — causing the people of the LORD to transgress.'

'If a man sins against a man, God shall judge for him; but if against the LORD a man sins, who shall pray for him?' But they did not listen to the voice of their father, because the LORD desired to put them to death.
And the boy Samuel was walking on and growing great and good, both with the LORD and with men.
A man of God came to Eli and said to him, 'Thus says the LORD: Did I not surely reveal myself to the house of your father when they were in Egypt for the house of Pharaoh?'

'And I chose him from all the tribes of Israel for myself to be a priest, to go up upon my altar, to burn incense, to bear an ephod before my face — and I gave to the house of your father all the offerings by fire of the sons of Israel.'

'Why will you kick at my sacrifice and at my offering that I commanded for the dwelling place? And you have honored your sons more than me, to make yourselves fat from the firstfruits of every offering of Israel my people.'

'Therefore, declaration of the LORD God of Israel: I surely said that your house and the house of your father would walk before my face forever. But now, declaration of the LORD: far be it from me, for those who honor me I will honor, and those who despise me will be lightly esteemed.'
'Behold, days are coming, and I will cut off your arm and the arm of the house of your father — so that there will not be an old man in your house.'
'And you shall look upon the distress of the dwelling place, in all that he does good to Israel — and there shall not be an old man in your house all the days.'
'A man I will not cut off for you from at my altar — to consume your eyes and to grieve your soul; and all the increase of your house shall die as men of age.'
'And this for you is the sign that shall come to your two sons, to Hophni and Phinehas: on one day they shall both die.'

'And I will raise up for myself a faithful priest — according to what is in my heart and in my soul he will do — and I will build him a faithful house, and he will walk before the face of my anointed all the days.'

'And it shall be every one remaining in your house will come to bow down to him for a piece of silver and a loaf of bread, and shall say, Attach me now to one of the priesthoods, that I may eat a piece of bread.'
